flashcards.front: What is the primary purpose of an abstract class in Java?
flashcards.back: To provide a common base with shared implementation for a group of related classes, while forcing subclasses to implement specific methods.

flashcards.front: What is the key characteristic of an abstract method in Java?
flashcards.back: An abstract method is declared with the abstract keyword and has no body, requiring subclasses to provide an implementation.

flashcards.front: Can an abstract class in Java have both abstract and concrete methods?
flashcards.back: Yes, an abstract class can have a mix of abstract methods (no body) and concrete methods (with body).

flashcards.front: What is the main difference between an abstract class and an interface in Java?
flashcards.back: An abstract class can provide a common base with shared implementation and state, while an interface defines a pure contract with no implementation or state.

flashcards.front: When should you use an abstract class instead of an interface in Java?
flashcards.back: Use an abstract class when you need to provide a common base with shared implementation and state, or when you want to force subclasses to implement specific methods.

flashcards.front: Can you instantiate an abstract class directly in Java?
flashcards.back: No, you cannot instantiate an abstract class directly; you must create a concrete subclass.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the abstract keyword in Java?
flashcards.back: The abstract keyword is used to declare an abstract class or method, indicating that it cannot be instantiated or has no implementation, respectively.
